name: Dubrovnik
summary: Dubrovnik is consistently ranked as Europe's safest city and one of the world's most secure tourist destinations. With extremely low violent crime rates, well-maintained infrastructure, and excellent emergency services, travelers can explore this UNESCO World Heritage city with confidence.
quickVerdict: Dubrovnik is remarkably safe for tourists, earning the top safety ranking among European cities in 2025. The city maintains a crime index of just 8.76 (very low), with violent crime being almost non-existent and petty crime rates 62% lower than Rome. The tourism-focused economy means security is prioritized, with well-lit streets, efficient police response times of 4.2 minutes, and comprehensive safety infrastructure throughout the Old Town and surrounding areas.

neighborhoods.name: Old Town (Stari Grad)
neighborhoods.description: The UNESCO World Heritage old town is exceptionally safe with constant foot traffic, well-lit cobblestone streets, and regular security patrols. Car-free environment makes it ideal for walking day and night.

neighborhoods.name: Lapad Peninsula
neighborhoods.description: Family-friendly residential and hotel area with excellent safety record. Popular beaches, restaurants, and accommodation with good lighting and regular public transport connections.

neighborhoods.name: Ploce
neighborhoods.description: Upscale area east of Old Town featuring luxury hotels and restaurants. Well-maintained with good security presence and stunning views, though more expensive than other areas.

neighborhoods.name: Gruž Port
neighborhoods.description: Main ferry and cruise port with busy commercial activity. Generally safe but requires normal precautions around transport hubs. Well-connected by public transport.

neighborhoods.name: Babin Kuk
neighborhoods.description: Resort peninsula with high-end hotels and maintained beaches. Enhanced security due to luxury accommodations, though can be quiet outside hotel areas in off-season.

neighborhoods.name: Konavle Region
neighborhoods.description: Rural area southeast of Dubrovnik with some remaining landmine risk from 1990s conflict. Beautiful countryside but visitors should stick to marked paths and avoid dense forest areas.

scams.name: Taxi Overcharging
scams.description: Some taxi drivers may quote inflated prices to tourists, especially at the airport or during peak season. Meters may not be used or routes may be unnecessarily long.
scams.howToAvoid: Use Uber or Bolt when available, agree on fare before starting, or use official airport buses. Licensed taxis should have meters running.

scams.name: Restaurant Bill Padding
scams.description: Occasional instances of extra items being added to bills at tourist restaurants, particularly in prime Old Town locations during high season.
scams.howToAvoid: Always check your receipt carefully, ensure menu prices are clearly displayed, and don't hesitate to question charges you don't recognize.

scams.name: Fake Boat Tour Operators
scams.description: Unlicensed boat operators may approach tourists offering private tours without proper insurance, safety equipment, or permits, potentially leaving you stranded or unsafe.
scams.howToAvoid: Book tours through reputable agencies, verify boat licenses and captain certificates, ensure life jackets are provided, and avoid last-minute dock approaches.

scams.name: Grocery Store Scanning Scam
scams.description: Rare instances of cashiers at tourist area stores adding extra items to bills, expecting tourists won't notice or understand the receipt.
scams.howToAvoid: Watch items being scanned, check receipts before leaving, and politely question any charges that seem incorrect. Most stores are honest and will correct mistakes.

soloFemale.overview: Dubrovnik ranks as the number one safest destination for solo female travelers globally, with a safety rating of 4.9/5. Women consistently report feeling completely comfortable walking alone both day and night, with harassment being extremely rare. The well-lit, pedestrian-friendly Old Town and strong local respect for visitors create an ideal environment for independent female travel.
tips: The Old Town is exceptionally safe for solo exploration with constant foot traffic and excellent lighting
Public transport is reliable and safe even late at night, with routes operating until past midnight
Local attitudes are respectful and welcoming, with most unwanted attention coming from intoxicated tourists rather than locals
Emergency call boxes are positioned every kilometer on popular hiking trails like Srđ Hill
Join walking tours or cooking classes to meet other travelers and locals in a safe, structured environment

nightSafety.overview: Dubrovnik maintains exceptional safety after dark, with the Old Town and main areas remaining well-lit and populated until late hours. The city's nightlife is sophisticated rather than rowdy, centered around atmospheric bars and restaurants. Crime rates remain consistently low at night, and the tourism police maintain visible presence in key areas.
tips: Stradun and main squares remain lively and safe with good lighting and regular foot traffic until late
Cliff bars like Buža offer stunning views but watch your drinks - 13% of visitors noted drink-spiking attempts
Use well-lit paths when walking between venues and avoid deserted alleys after midnight
Reliable taxi services and Uber operate 24/7 for safe transport back to accommodation
Clubs like Revelin fortress maintain 87% visitor satisfaction for safe nightlife experiences

transport.metro: No metro system. Dubrovnik relies on buses, taxis, and walking for local transport.
transport.rideshare: Uber and Bolt operate reliably with good safety records. Drivers undergo background checks and app provides tracking features. Generally cheaper than taxis except during surge pricing.
transport.taxis: Licensed white taxis with red stripes are generally safe but can be expensive. Some overcharging of tourists occurs, so agree on price beforehand or ensure meter is running. Available 24/7.
transport.tips: The Old Town is car-free and best explored on foot. Public buses (Libertas) are safe and run until past midnight. Airport shuttle buses are reliable and affordable at €7.

customs: Greet with firm handshake, direct eye contact, and 'Dobar dan' (Good day) - punctuality is valued in professional settings
When invited to homes, bring small gifts like wine or flowers (odd numbers only, avoid even numbers as they're for funerals)
Remove shoes when entering homes and accept offered coffee or food to avoid offense - refusal is seen as rejecting hospitality

health.overview: Healthcare quality in Dubrovnik is good with both public and private options available. The main General Hospital provides 24/7 emergency care, while private clinics offer faster service for non-emergencies. Many medical staff speak English, especially in tourist areas.
health.water: Tap water is completely safe to drink throughout Dubrovnik and often considered among the best in Croatia. You can refill bottles at public fountains like Big Onofrio's Fountain in the Old Town.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations required for Croatia. Routine vaccines like MMR, flu, and COVID-19 should be up to date. No malaria or yellow fever risk.
health.altitude: Sea level location with Mediterranean climate. Summer temperatures can reach 30°C (86°F) - use adequate SPF and stay hydrated. No altitude-related health concerns.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Embassy Zagreb: +385 1 661 2300 (after hours emergency). Embassy located at Ulica Thomasa Jeffersona 2, Zagreb. For life-threatening emergencies involving US citizens.

faq.a: Yes, Dubrovnik is exceptionally safe and ranked as Europe's safest city in 2025. With very low crime rates, excellent emergency services, and tourism-focused security infrastructure, visitors can explore confidently day and night.

Absolutely. The Old Town remains well-lit and populated until late hours, with efficient police response times of 4.2 minutes. Main areas like Stradun are particularly safe, though basic precautions should still be taken.

faq.q: Is Dubrovnik safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Dubrovnik ranks #1 globally for solo female travel safety. Women report feeling completely comfortable walking alone, with harassment being extremely rare and usually from other tourists rather than locals.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Dubrovnik?
faq.a: No specific areas need to be avoided in Dubrovnik itself. The Konavle region has some historical landmine risk in dense forests, and rural Karlovac/Lika-Senj counties have increased caution advisories, but tourist areas are completely safe.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Dubrovnik?
faq.a: Yes, public buses are very safe and reliable, operating until past midnight. Uber and licensed taxis are also safe options. The Old Town is pedestrian-only and best explored on foot.
